text:
Name:  Smith, Elizabeth                       Unit No:  0b1d88ec-cb1c-304a-9f43-53bfc0614e63

Admission Date:  2018-10-17     Discharge Date:  1988-12-10

Date of Birth:   1970-08-04     Sex:  M

Service:

ADDENDUM:  This is an Addendum to a prior dictated Discharge
Summary with job number [**Job Number **].

From the prior dictated Discharge Summary, the patient was
discharged on 1988-12-10.  The patient's event included a
slightly elevated white blood cell count around 15 to 17.
Given the patient's elevated white blood cell count, the
patient was not discharged to a rehabilitation facility at
that time.  Instead, he was monitored in the hospital for a
few more days.  The patient was pan-cultured, and all
cultures were negative.

A computed tomography scan of his abdomen was repeated.  The
computed tomography scan was negative for any abscess or
obstruction.

The patient's white blood cell count was monitored daily.
His white blood cell count slowly normalized over a period of
three days.  The patient remained asymptomatic and had
adequate ostomy output.

Given that the patient was afebrile and his vital signs were
stable and was asymptomatic, it was thought that the patient
could be discharged home on postoperative day 22.  The
patient refused to be sent to a rehabilitation facility;
although that had been highly recommended.  He was seen by
Physical Therapy who cleared him for discharge on the day of
his discharge.  He was set up to have home physical therapy
as well as visiting nurses to check his wound and ostomy.
The patient was discharged to home in good condition.

The patient was not discharged home on fluconazole given that
he had finished his course.  He was only sent out on
pantoprazole and Vicodin.  He was told to resume his pre
hospitalization medications.  The patient was also instructed
to follow up with Dr. Lindsay Acosta in two to three weeks.





                          [**First Name11 (Name Pattern1) **] Glass, M.D.

Dictated By:Nicholas Moore

MEDQUIST36
